introSection: Cyclone is a wooden roller coaster currently standing but not operating at Lakeside Amusement Park in Denver, Colorado, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
On August 2, 2003 American Coaster Enthusiasts recognized the ride as an ACE Roller Coaster Landmark.

In 2021 the coaster was temporarily closed during the second half of the season.

Cyclone operated from the start of the 2022 season until June when it was ordered to close by state officials.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The trains were originally built by Vettel and would be replaced with trains built in-house at an unknown time.

sections.title: Incident
sections.content: Two trains collided on May 14, 1972. Four riders suffered minor injuries. The ride was later cleared to reopen by a Colorado safety inspector and was set to reopen on June 11. In July, two of the injured riders filed a $225,000 suit against the park.

fullText:
Cyclone is a wooden roller coaster currently standing but not operating at Lakeside Amusement Park in Denver, Colorado, USA.

On August 2, 2003 American Coaster Enthusiasts recognized the ride as an ACE Roller Coaster Landmark.

In 2021 the coaster was temporarily closed during the second half of the season.

Cyclone operated from the start of the 2022 season until June when it was ordered to close by state officials.



White track and supports.

The trains were originally built by Vettel and would be replaced with trains built in-house at an unknown time.

Two trains collided on May 14, 1972. Four riders suffered minor injuries. The ride was later cleared to reopen by a Colorado safety inspector and was set to reopen on June 11. In July, two of the injured riders filed a $225,000 suit against the park.
